300 items across three paradigms: procedural memory (one-shot skill retention after interference), priming (theme-driven response biases), classical conditioning (CS-US exposure shaping first decisions). First benchmark targeting implicit (non-declarative) memory in LLMs; exposes a category of memory failure invisible to existing explicit-memory benchmarks.

5 core memory abilities — info extraction, multi-session reasoning, temporal reasoning, knowledge updates, abstention — via 500 curated questions in synthetic chat histories. Two scales: LongMemEval-S (115K tokens), LongMemEval-M (up to 1.5M).

Renmin University. Evaluates LLM-agent memory mechanisms across three axes — effectiveness (accuracy), efficiency (LLM call count), capacity (degradation as memory grows). Distinguishes factual vs reflective memory, participation vs observation scenarios.

QA tasks across 7 domains / 36 risk types plus workflow tasks adapted from 20 Agent-SafetyBench environments with noisy tool returns; both use mixed benign-and-misleading memory pools in multi-round interactions. Targets memory-evolution safety, not recall accuracy — first benchmark specifically targeting adversarial degradation of evolving memory.
Addresses a largely unexamined attack surface — adversarial memory injection into agents with evolving long-term stores — rather than standard retrieval accuracy.
Very recent; limited replication and adoption.

Preprint; arena methodology has known biases at small sample sizes.

Benchmarks memory in realistic Memory-Agent-Environment loops where tasks span multiple sessions and subtasks are explicitly interdependent — agents must distill prior-session experiences into memory and use that memory to act later. memory tool docs Auto Dream guide memory import tool Wiegold guide
Three-tier model (memory tool API + consumer Memory + Auto Dream) covers developer, user, and system layers; document-as-memory unit aligns with how humans organize information.
Memory granularity and eviction policy not yet documented publicly; filesystem-as-memory has known scalability limits at large knowledge volumes; beta status means API surface may change.

Mounts memory as files on a managed filesystem; Claude reads and writes via the same bash/code-execution tools used for agentic tasks. Unit of storage is a file — inspectable, editable, exportable by developers. Full audit log per session. Memory beta launched April 23, 2026, two weeks after Managed Agents itself launched April 8.
Filesystem metaphor aligns with Claude's existing tool-use primitives; no separate embedding pipeline; human-auditable without special tooling.
AWS-only; pricing tied to Bedrock; cross-cloud portability requires rework.
AWS managed memory service for agents running on Bedrock AgentCore. Native integrations with LangChain/LangGraph and Strands. Drives long-running MCP servers + agent workloads.
AgentCore Memory docs long-running MCP + Strands
Native to Bedrock with deep integration into IAM, KMS, CloudWatch — enterprise compliance posture is built in.
Cloudflare-only; cold-start latency for less-frequented edges; ecosystem still maturing.
Managed persistent memory service for AI agents on Cloudflare's edge. Beta-launched April 2026. Sits alongside Workers AI / Vectorize as the memory tier of the Cloudflare agent stack.
Edge-deployed agent memory — geographically distributed by default; integrates with Workers / D1 / Durable Objects.
Cross-product data sharing is a trust liability; persistence model has been less clearly communicated than ChatGPT's.
Personal Context setting + Personal Intelligence packing. Memory import from ChatGPT/Claude (Mar 2026).
DataStudios writeup enterprise personalization privacy controls blog
Tightest integration of any memory product with personal data sources (Gmail, Drive, Calendar) where users have meaningful history.
Privacy framing is weakest of the major platforms; corpus quality varies sharply by user activity.
Extracts and persists facts from past conversations; applies them to future responses. Per-memory deletion UI; user can also clear all.
Tied to X timeline data — a corpus no other vendor has access to.
Closed ecosystem with no developer access; memory depth limited by what users actually post.

Production memory infrastructure powering LinkedIn's Hiring Assistant and other GenAI apps. Three-layer (episodic/semantic/procedural) shared memory substrate across multi-agent systems; recent-context retrieval + semantic search + summarisation-based compaction. April 2026.
LinkedIn engineering blog InfoQ writeup ZenML LLMOps DB
First professional-network memory with a structured domain corpus (job history, connections, posts) — most other products see only freeform chat.
Non-developer surface only; no API; cross-product data flow remains a privacy concern.

Stores facts from one-on-one chats and shares them across Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger, Facebook when accounts are linked via Accounts Center. The widest cross-surface memory scope of any consumer product — propagates context across entirely separate social apps, not just devices/browsers.
Native delivery via WhatsApp / Instagram / Messenger reaches billions at the app level.
Tied to M365 — non-MS users have no path; feature cadence slowed by enterprise compliance review.
Microsoft 365 Copilot memory stored in user's Exchange mailbox with compliance alignment. GA Jul 2025.
Tech Community announcement Microsoft Learn docs
Enterprise-ready by default — M365 tenant boundaries, audit trails, compliance certifications; Pages abstraction makes memory inspectable like a document.
Closed infrastructure; no published architecture detail on how memory is stored/retrieved/evicted; no peer-reviewed evaluation of memory accuracy or long-term drift.

Mistral's Agents API provides persistent conversation state across turns and sessions; runtime maintains branching conversation trees. Memory is implicit in the stateful session model rather than a separate store — agents can branch, continue prior discussions, and accumulate tool-call history without manual tracking.
Fully managed — developers need no external state store for session continuity; branching conversations allow parallel reasoning paths.
Smaller ecosystem and slower feature cadence than US competitors; memory documentation is sparse.

Le Chat Memories — graph-based, context-aware. Stores user preferences and past decisions; memories saved explicitly (user instruction) or inferred automatically. Coupled to "Work Mode" agentic layer.
First major European platform with native memory; positioning emphasizes data residency and EU compliance.
Memory is opaque — no inspect/edit primitive beyond the settings UI; cross-thread leakage has been a recurring privacy concern.
First-party memory in ChatGPT; auto-extracts persistent facts about the user across sessions. Apr 2025 expansion to chat-history referencing.
launch announcement memory FAQ how it works (EmbraceTheRed)
Largest installed base of memory-using AI users by orders of magnitude; cross-conversation context reads chat history automatically since Sept 2025.
Primarily helps repeat-search scenarios; less useful as general assistive memory than competitors.

Auto-infers facts from conversations and surfaces them in future responses (Pro subscribers). Cross-model: memories persist when user switches underlying model mid-session. Enterprise Memory adds org-scoped retrieval.
Memory layered onto a search-grounded answer engine means recalled facts arrive with citations by construction.
GCP-only; pricing tied to Vertex; smaller mind-share than AWS / Anthropic memory.
Google Cloud managed memory service (Preview). Used by ADK's VertexAIMemoryBankService for cross-session persistent memory. Intelligently stores and retrieves memories from past user interactions.
Google Cloud-native managed memory with strong RAG integrations and enterprise governance posture.

current_value: Continuous Thought Machines. Each neuron maintains its own history of incoming activations via private per-neuron weight parameters; the model operates along a self-generated "thought step" timeline rather than the input sequence timeline. Neural synchronization across neurons is used directly as a latent representation. Distinct from Sakana NAMM (evolutionary attention memory) — CTM is an architectural rethink of the recurrent state itself.

Neural long-term memory module that learns to memorise at test time. Uses gradient-of-loss as "surprise" signal; adaptive weight-decay forgetting. Three variants: MAC (memory-as-context), MAG (memory-as-gate), MAL (memory-as-layer).
